exemplar.title: An Investigation of Numerical Methods of Integration Using 1/ln x

detailedExemplarData.title: An Investigation of Numerical Methods of Integration Using 1/ln x

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear logical progression with introduction, method sections and conclusion
Well-labelled headings, subheadings, tables and figures aiding navigation
Content is largely relevant, focusing on comparison of numerical methods
weaknesses: Minor typographical slips and over-long derivations interrupt flow
Some figures and tables poorly formatted or placed
Occasional repetition and digressions reduce conciseness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands were assessed at band 3: the investigation is coherent and logically developed, well organized with clear structure, and mostly concise with relevant content. Minor formatting and conciseness issues prevent a band 4, so a score of 3 is appropriate.

strengths: Consistent use of standard notation (e.g. Î£, Îx, ln x) throughout
Effective integration of tables, graphs and derivations to support arguments
Logical flow from formula derivation to numerical evaluation and interpretation
weaknesses: Occasional notation inconsistencies (dt/dx mix, bold vs. non-bold symbols)
Graphs and tables sometimes compressed with small axes labels
Lengthy proofs would benefit from tighter phrasing
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Each mathematical communication strand sits at band 3: mostly consistent use of notation, effective (but not highly effective) multiple representations, and good logical structure and clarity. Minor inconsistencies and presentation issues keep the criterion at 3 rather than 4.

strengths: Clear evidence of independent thinking in choosing a non-syllabus integral and deriving Simpsonâs rule
Personal motivation (TMUA preparation) is evident and well-articulated
Creative initiative shown through comparison of four numerical methods
weaknesses: Investigation path remains largely conventional without pushing into adaptive methods or advanced error analysis
Personal voice is present but not highly innovative
Could further personalize by exploring novel extensions or alternative integrals
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All personal engagement strands are at band 2: significant evidence of independent thinking, clear personal approach, and good creativity. The work does not reach the highest band, so a score of 2 reflects this level.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on why certain rules outperform others
Thoughtful analysis of midpoint vs. rectangle and Simpsonâs superiority
Clear evaluation by ranking methods and noting conditions of failure
weaknesses: Lacks deeper discussion of wider implications or rigorous error-term analysis
Future work and computational efficiency considerations are only lightly touched upon
Reflection remains at surface level without exploring advanced theoretical contexts
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection strands all meet band 2: meaningful reflection throughout, thoughtful analysis of results and process, and clear evaluation of outcomes. The absence of substantial critical reflection or comprehensive future implications precludes a band 3, so 2 marks are awarded.

strengths: Relevant mathematics (four numerical integration techniques plus derivation) used appropriately
Mathematics commensurate with SL course level and correctly applied
Procedures (Îx computation, percentageâerror formula) are accurate and clearly displayed
weaknesses: Range of mathematics, while solid, is not comprehensive (no error-bound analysis or series approximations)
Relies on external GDC value for 'exact' integral without error-bound discussion
Minor notation inconsistencies prevent completely flawless execution
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The use of mathematics is clearly appropriate and effective (band 4). Although the student shows thorough understanding and accurate application, the investigation does not offer a comprehensive range of mathematical tools or rigorous error analysis to justify a higher band. A score of 4 reflects strong but not expert use of mathematics.

strandSelections.reasoning: Arguments are generally clear: each formula is followed by a substitution, evaluation and reflection on accuracy.  Paragraphing is logical; however, some proofs (e.g. trapezoid equivalence) are lengthy and would benefit from tighter phrasing.  Clarity is good but falls short of the âexcellentâ descriptor.

The student demonstrates thorough understanding by correctly applying each rule, deriving Simpsonâs formula, and computing percentage errors accurately over multiple n-values.  Small notation inconsistencies remain, preventing a âcompletely correctâ (6-mark) award, but conceptual understanding is clearly thorough and predominantly error-free.

Graphs, tables of percentage error and algebraic derivations are integrated effectively to support arguments.  While these representations are helpful, their explanatory power is occasionally limited by small axes or compressed tables, so use is âeffectiveâ rather than âhighly effectiveâ.

The investigation progresses in a logical order â introduction, four numerical methods, comparison, conclusion â and each section links smoothly to the next.  Explanations of why a new technique is introduced (e.g.
Rectangle â Midpoint â Trapezium â Simpson) create clear argumentative continuity.  Minor digressions, typographical slips and the occasional over-long derivation prevent the work from reaching the âexcellentâ level, but overall coherence is consistently good.

Deriving Simpsonâs Rule from first principles and comparing four numerical techniques across many sub-intervals demonstrates creativity and initiative.  Yet the investigation does not extend to, for example, adaptive step sizing or error-bound analysis, so creativity is good but not exceptional.

There is thoughtful analysis of why the Midpoint Rule is more accurate than the Rectangle Rule and why Simpsonâs Rule excels, but the critique stops at surface observations; no rigorous error-term analysis or consideration of computational efficiency is attempted.  This meets the âthoughtful analysisâ descriptor.

Outcomes are evaluated by ranking methods and noting conditions where Simpsonâs Rule may fail, demonstrating clear evaluation.  However, suggestions for future work are limited and potential sources of numerical or rounding error are not fully explored, keeping the work in band 2.

The student regularly comments on whether results are âsurprisingâ or âas expectedâ and links percentage error to number of sub-intervals, showing meaningful reflection.  Reflection is present throughout but lacks deeper discussion of the wider implications of numerical integration, so it sits in the middle band.

Standard notation (Îx, Î£, PE, ln x) is used consistently in the main text and proofs; a few slips (mixed dt/dx, bold u) show minor inconsistency.  Overall, mathematical language is appropriate and mostly accurate, fitting the âmostly consistentâ band.

The exploration is clearly motivated by the studentâs TMUA preparation and curiosity about âunsolvableâ integrals.  The personal voice is evident in choice of interval and commentary on unexpected results, but the investigative pathway remains conventional, giving âclear evidenceâ of a personal approach without being highly innovative.

Nearly all material relates to comparing numerical methods, yet repetitive step-by-step calculations for several values of n and lengthy historical remarks add bulk without new insight.  Content is largely relevant but not always concise, matching the âconcise with mostly relevant contentâ description.

All mathematics directly addresses the aim: definitions, proofs and error calculations are relevant and used effectively.  The range (four numerical methods plus a derivation) is solid but not especially wide, so the work is âeffectiveâ rather than âcomprehensiveâ.

The student chooses an integral outside the syllabus and independently derives Simpsonâs Rule, showing notable personal initiative but largely following standard textbook methods.  This reflects âsignificantâ rather than âoutstandingâ independent thinking.

Headings, sub-headings, numbered tables and labelled figures give the piece a clear structure; the reader can locate definitions, proofs and results quickly.  There is a recognizable introduction and conclusion.  Some captioning and reference formatting are inconsistent, so the organization, while strong, is not quite âexceptionalâ.

Numerical integration is fully commensurate with HL AA content and the derivation of Simpsonâs Rule slightly extends the syllabus.  While rigorous, the mathematics does not venture into university-level analysis of error bounds, so the work fits the descriptor âfully commensurate with course levelâ.

annotations.comment: The title is concise and descriptive, clearly indicating the focus on numerical integration of $\frac{1}{\ln x}$, supporting logical development and reader engagement.
The introduction effectively relates the logarithmic integral to prime-counting and applications, demonstrating a genuine personal curiosity and context.

After substitution the new integral $\int e^u/u\,du$ is obtained correctly, but reflection could deepen by introducing the exponential integral function $\mathrm{Ei}(u)$ or discussing why an elementary antiderivative is impossible.
The inline bold $\boldsymbol{u}$ is inconsistent with previous notation; maintain uniform symbol styles to avoid confusion.
Linking the investigation to TMUA exam preparation shows clear personal engagement and genuine motivation for exploring numerical methods.
The aim statement is clear and explicitly defines the target integral and interval, providing strong structural guidance for the investigation.
The integral notation $\displaystyle\int_2^3\frac{1}{\ln x}\,dx$ is used correctly and consistently, aiding mathematical clarity.
Figure 1âs axes and labels are small and compressed, which limits interpretability; consider enlarging labels and adjusting margins.
Relying solely on a Desmos value for the âexactâ integral omits discussion of potential numerical error; include an error bound or compare with a series approximation.
The âRiemann Sumsâ section header is clearly labeled, enhancing overall organization and guiding the reader through the methodology.
The derivation of the Riemann sum definition shows logical progression from partition to summation, reinforcing understanding.
Before introducing $\Delta x_i$, clarify the uniform partition assumption explicitly to strengthen mathematical communication.
Equation $\displaystyle\int_a^b f(x)dx=\sum f(x_i)\Delta x_i$ is correctly stated but could benefit from a note on index limits ($i=1\ldots n$) for precision.
Figure 2âs visual aid effectively illustrates Riemann sums, but its large size overlaps text; adjusting scale and placement would improve clarity.
A brief comparative remark on how midpoint partitions improve on rectangle estimates would enhance reflective depth.
Labelling the 'Rectangle Rule' subsection clearly aids navigation and reinforces the documentâs structure.
The paragraph explaining expected over/under-approximation is dense; consider splitting into shorter sentences for better readability.
The computation of $\Delta x=\frac{3-2}{5}=0.2$ is accurate and clearly displayed, demonstrating strong procedural understanding.
Before calculating heights, a reminder of the error sign (positive/negative) for decreasing functions would reinforce conceptual understanding.
Figure 3âs caption placement below the graph is nonstandard; move captions above or immediately adjacent to improve reader orientation.
The percentage error formula is applied correctly and concisely, demonstrating precise numerical reasoning.
Including the GDC approximation is valuable, but specify calculator settings or commands to ensure reproducibility.
Specify sample points as $x_i^*$ to align with Riemann sum convention and improve notation consistency.
The comparison between left and right endpoint errors is clearly articulated, providing insight into approximation behaviors.
Table 1 formatting shows valuable data but title font and alignment are inconsistent; harmonize styling with other tables.
Averaging left and right endpoint estimates is justified well, illustrating thoughtful error cancellation reasoning.
The concise derivation of average percentage error reinforces accuracy analysis, maintaining mathematical precision.
Figure 5âs multiple traces are helpful but color overlap reduces clarity; use distinct markers or line styles.
The discussion of convergence as $n\to\infty$ is insightful; expanding with Big-O error terms would deepen critical analysis.
Clarify the theoretical error order for left vs right endpoints when stating $LE>RE$ to reinforce why one bound is better.
